chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No. D- 272 of 2026 (Nek Muhammad and another vs. P.O Sindh and others Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.961/2026 (U/A). 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on CMA No.962/2026 (Ex/A). 4. For the hearing of the main case. 5. For orders on CMA No.963/2026 (S/A). 11.02.2026. Mr. Yar Khan Shambani, Advocate for petitioners. -.-.-.- Through this petition, the petitioner has prayed that the notices issued by the Revenue authorities for fresh demarcation be declared illegal and that the respondents be restrained from conducting any such demarcation. Learned counsel for the petitioner advanced submissions on the aforesaid aspect. However, it is settled law that where an adequate and efficacious remedy is available under the relevant Revenue laws, the Constitutional jurisdiction of this Court, being extraordinary in nature, is to be exercised sparingly and in exceptional circumstances only. The petitioner has not availed of the remedy provided under the Revenue hierarchy. Furthermore, the Public Grievances Redressal Cell has also been established to address such kind of issues. It is a well-recognized principle that when an alternate statutory remedy is available, the Constitutional jurisdiction is ordinarily not to be invoked. In these circumstances, as an adequate and efficacious remedy is available to the petitioner under the law, the instant petition is not maintainable. Reliance in this regard is placed upon the judgments reported as Indus Trading and Contracting Company v. Collector of Customs (Preventive), Karachi and others (2016 SCMR 842); President, All Pakistan Women Association, Peshawar Cantt. v.

Reliance in this regard is placed upon the judgments reported as Indus Trading and Contracting Company v. Collector of Customs (Preventive), Karachi and others (2016 SCMR 842); President, All Pakistan Women Association, Peshawar Cantt. v. 2 Muhammad Akbar Awan and others (2020 SCMR 260); Commissioner Inland Revenue and others v. Jahangir Khan Tareen and others (2022 SCMR 92); and Executive Director (P&GS), State Life, Principal Office Karachi and others v. Muhammad Nisar, Area Manager, State Life Corporation of Pakistan, Peshawar Zone (2025 SCMR 249). Keeping in view the above facts and circumstances, this petition, being without merit, is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE
